symptoms.symptom_name: Traumatic avulsion of the wound dressing during a bed transfer

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatically dislodged wound dressing (skin-graft donor site)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear mechanical trigger explains the sudden exposure, ooze and pain without an infective or deep-tissue cause.

symptoms.character: steady uniform weep over the whole raw area, staining linen, no spurting

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatically dislodged wound dressing (skin-graft donor site)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Capillary ooze from raw dermis is the expected bleeding pattern for an exposed donor site.
Non-pulsatile, uniform bleeding without a spurting point excludes an arterial source.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe burning/stinging pain over the exposed surface, relieved by covering
symptoms.character: burning, stinging, diffuse; eased when covered

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatically dislodged wound dressing (skin-graft donor site)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exposed dermal nociceptors produce intense air-exposure pain relieved by occlusion — characteristic of an open donor site.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of fever, rigors, spreading erythema, purulent exudate or systemic illness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative systemic and local infection screen makes established wound infection unlikely at presentation and downgrades the tier from emergency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Large raw oozing donor-site surface visible on video (~10 cm), blood-stained linen

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatically dislodged wound dressing (skin-graft donor site)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Video-observable large raw surface is consistent with an exposed superficial donor site; extent looks dramatic but is superficial.

symptoms.symptom_name: Healthy pink granulation, pinpoint bleeding wound bed, no slough or necrosis; surrounding skin with only mild healing erythema, no maceration or spreading cellulitis

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Traumatically dislodged wound dressing (skin-graft donor site)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician wound-bed assessment confirms healthy healing tissue and excludes infection/necrosis — a sealed hands-on finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.
Absence of slough, necrosis, maceration or spreading erythema on direct inspection argues against infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Baseline wound swab for microscopy, culture and sensitivity (taken due to environmental exposure)

investigation_details.expected_result: no significant pathogenic growth expected; serial wound measurements should show steady reduction over time
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A baseline swab is prudent given exposure but is not urgently diagnostic; it is a hands-on/lab step unavailable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A swab retrospectively characterises any colonising organisms; a negative culture supports uncomplicated healing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Facility-nurse-relayed vitals: BP ~140/85, HR ~102, temp 37.0 C, SpO2 98%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile with only mild pain-related tachycardia and normal saturations — no physiological sign of sepsis; supports a non-emergency tier.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Benign exposed donor site with capillary ooze
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Traumatically dislodged wound dressing (skin-graft donor site)
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Mechanical trigger + non-pulsatile ooze + air-exposure pain + negative infection screen together define a benign, non-emergency wound needing prompt hands-on re-dressing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline wound swab M/C/S at the in-person re-dressing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reasonable given environmental exposure, to be taken by the attending nurse/GP; not an emergency investigation and not obtainable via telehealth.
No systemic features — routine bloods are not indicated and should not be over-ordered for a clean, systemically-well wound.
